ucs.title: [LLM] BlackByte BYOVD: RTCore64.sys (MSI Afterburner) driver dropped to disable EDR
ucs.description: Hunts BlackByte's signature 'Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver' tradecraft — the actor drops the legitimately-signed MSI Afterburner driver RTCore64.sys (CVE-2019-16098) and abuses its IOCTL interface to terminate EDR/AV processes from kernel-mode before launching encryption. The combination of a non-MSI process writing RTCore64.sys outside Program Files, followed by it being registered/loaded as a kernel driver, is highly specific to BlackByte and a small set of imitators (HermeticWiper, Scattered Spider).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.process_name) as writer_process from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="RTCore64.sys" OR Filesystem.file_name="rtcore64.sys" OR Filesystem.file_hash IN ("01aa278b07b58dc46c84bd0b1b5c8e9ee4e62ea0bf7a695e7315d6e6e0e1d6f6","0a01a87a19fcfa37810043ec38de5cebf1ef3577b5cba0bce6a3e2eed8d6fc25")) by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.process_id host | rename Filesystem.* as * | where NOT (file_path LIKE "%\\Program Files\\MSI Afterburner\\%" OR file_path LIKE "%\\Program Files (x86)\\MSI Afterburner\\%") | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Services where (Services.service_name="RTCore64" OR Services.service_path="*RTCore64.sys") by Services.dest Services.service_name Services.user _time | rename Services.* as * | rename _time as service_install_time] | eval gap_sec=abs(service_install_time-lastTime) | where gap_sec<3600 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime dest user file_name file_path writer_process service_name service_install_time gap_sec
ucs.kql:
// BlackByte BYOVD — RTCore64.sys dropped + loaded outside the legitimate MSI Afterburner install path
let Lookback = 14d;
let RTCoreHashes = dynamic([
    "01aa278b07b58dc46c84bd0b1b5c8e9ee4e62ea0bf7a695e7315d6e6e0e1d6f6",  // MSI RTCore64 v1.x
    "0a01a87a19fcfa37810043ec38de5cebf1ef3577b5cba0bce6a3e2eed8d6fc25"
]);
let DriverDrops =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
    | where FileName =~ "rtcore64.sys" or SHA256 in (RTCoreHashes)
    | where not(FolderPath has @"\Program Files\MSI Afterburner\")
        and not(FolderPath has @"\Program Files (x86)\MSI Afterburner\")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("msiexec.exe","setup.exe","MSIAfterburnerSetup.exe")
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              DroppedPath = FolderPath, DroppedSHA256 = SHA256,
              Writer = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              WriterCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              WriterAccount = InitiatingProcessAccountName;
let DriverLoads =
    DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where FileName =~ "rtcore64.sys"
    | project LoadTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, LoadedFolder = FolderPath,
              Loader = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              LoaderCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
DriverDrops
| join kind=inner DriverLoads on DeviceId
| where LoadTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 1h)
| extend DeltaSec = datetime_diff('second', LoadTime, DropTime)
| project DropTime, LoadTime, DeltaSec, DeviceName, WriterAccount,
          Writer, WriterCmd, DroppedPath, DroppedSHA256,
          Loader, LoaderCmd
| order by DropTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// BlackByte BYOVD — RTCore64.sys dropped + service install via ASIM file/registry events
let Lookback = 14d;
let Drops =
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
    | where TargetFileName =~ "rtcore64.sys"
    | where not(TargetFilePath has @"\Program Files\MSI Afterburner\")
        and not(TargetFilePath has @"\Program Files (x86)\MSI Afterburner\")
    | where ActingProcessName !in~ ("msiexec.exe","setup.exe","msiafterburnersetup.exe")
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              TargetFilePath, TargetFileSHA256,
              ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine;
let ServiceInstalls =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where EventID == 7045                       // service install
    | where ServiceName =~ "rtcore64" or ServiceFileName has "rtcore64.sys"
    | project SvcTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, ServiceName, ServiceFileName,
              ServiceStartType, SubjectAccount;
Drops
| join kind=inner ServiceInstalls on $left.DvcHostname == $right.Computer
| where SvcTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 1h)
| extend DeltaSec = datetime_diff('second', SvcTime, DropTime)
| project DropTime, SvcTime, DeltaSec, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine,
          TargetFilePath, TargetFileSHA256,
          ServiceName, ServiceFileName
| order by DropTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: BlackByte BYOVD - RTCore64.sys (MSI Afterburner) Dropped Outside Install Path
id: 6f4a3c9e-2b1f-4f92-9b2a-blackbyte-byovd-rtcore
status: experimental
description: Detects creation of the vulnerable MSI Afterburner driver RTCore64.sys outside the legitimate install directory - a hallmark of BlackByte ransomware EDR-kill tradecraft (CVE-2019-16098).
references:
    - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1043/
    - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa22-321a
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
    - attack.defense_evasion
    - attack.t1068
    - attack.t1562.001
    - attack.g1043
logsource:
    category: file_event
    product: windows
detection:
    selection:
        TargetFilename|endswith: '\RTCore64.sys'
    filter_msi_install:
        TargetFilename|contains:
            - '\Program Files\MSI Afterburner\'
            - '\Program Files (x86)\MSI Afterburner\'
    filter_legit_writer:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\msiexec.exe'
            - '\MSIAfterburnerSetup.exe'
    condition: selection and not 1 of filter_*
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate MSI Afterburner installation in non-default paths (rare)
level: high
ucs.rationale: RTCore64.sys-based EDR kill is documented as BlackByte's signature install-stage tradecraft in CISA AA22-321A and Sophos/Mandiant reporting. While other actors (Scattered Spider, LockBit affiliates) have copied the technique, the specific pattern of the driver being written by a non-installer process and immediately registered as a service is overwhelmingly BlackByte. Pairing the file drop with th

ucs.title: [LLM] BlackByte ransomware recovery-destruction chain (vssadmin + bcdedit + wbadmin)
ucs.description: Catches the BlackByte 2.0 pre-encryption sabotage sequence: vssadmin shadow-copy deletion → bcdedit ignoreallfailures/recoveryenabled No → wbadmin delete catalog, all spawned by the same root account on the same host within minutes. CISA AA22-321A documents this exact triplet as a BlackByte fingerprint distinct from Conti/LockBit, which use slightly different combinations.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as bins values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.user!="*$" AND ((Processes.process_name="vssadmin.exe" AND Processes.process IN ("*delete shadows*","*resize shadowstorage*")) OR (Processes.process_name="bcdedit.exe" AND (Processes.process="*ignoreallfailures*" OR Processes.process="*recoveryenabled*No*" OR Processes.process="*recoveryenabled No*")) OR (Processes.process_name="wbadmin.exe" AND Processes.process IN ("*delete catalog*","*delete systemstatebackup*")) OR (Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" AND Processes.process="*shadowcopy*delete*"))) by Processes.dest Processes.user span=10m | rename Processes.* as * | eval bin_count=mvcount(bins) | where bin_count>=2 | eval techniques=mvjoin(bins,",") | eval has_vssadmin=if(match(techniques,"vssadmin"),1,0) | eval has_bcdedit=if(match(techniques,"bcdedit"),1,0) | eval has_wbadmin=if(match(techniques,"wbadmin"),1,0) | eval blackbyte_score=has_vssadmin+has_bcdedit+has_wbadmin | where blackbyte_score>=2 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime dest user techniques cmdlines parents blackbyte_score
ucs.kql:
// BlackByte recovery-destruction triplet within 10-minute window
let Window = 10m;
let RecoveryKill =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | extend Tactic = case(
        FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("delete shadows","resize shadowstorage"), "vssadmin",
        FileName =~ "wmic.exe"     and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("shadowcopy","delete"),                  "wmic_vss",
        FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe"  and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("ignoreallfailures","recoveryenabled no","recoveryenabled No"), "bcdedit",
        FileName =~ "wbadmin.exe"  and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("delete catalog","delete systemstatebackup"), "wbadmin",
        "")
    | where isnotempty(Tactic)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              Tactic, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
              Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
RecoveryKill
| summarize FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen  = max(Timestamp),
            Tactics   = make_set(Tactic),
            Cmds      = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 50),
            Parents   = make_set(Parent),
            EvCount   = count()
            by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, bin(Timestamp, Window)
| extend DistinctTactics = array_length(Tactics)
| where DistinctTactics >= 2                       // 2/3 of the BlackByte triplet
   and Tactics has_any ("bcdedit","wbadmin")        // require at least one boot-/backup-attack signal
| project FirstSeen, LastSeen, DeviceName, AccountName,
          DistinctTactics, Tactics, Parents, Cmds, EvCount
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// BlackByte recovery-destruction triplet on Sentinel — SecurityEvent 4688 (requires CommandLine auditing)
let Window = 10m;
let RecoveryKill =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend Bin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | extend Tactic = case(
        Bin == "vssadmin.exe" and CommandLine has_any ("delete shadows","resize shadowstorage"), "vssadmin",
        Bin == "wmic.exe"     and CommandLine has_all ("shadowcopy","delete"),                  "wmic_vss",
        Bin == "bcdedit.exe"  and CommandLine has_any ("ignoreallfailures","recoveryenabled no","recoveryenabled No"), "bcdedit",
        Bin == "wbadmin.exe"  and CommandLine has_any ("delete catalog","delete systemstatebackup"), "wbadmin",
        "")
    | where isnotempty(Tactic)
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Tactic,
              NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
RecoveryKill
| summarize FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
            Tactics   = make_set(Tactic),
            Cmds      = make_set(CommandLine, 50),
            Parents   = make_set(ParentProcessName),
            EvCount   = count()
            by Computer, Account, bin(TimeGenerated, Window)
| extend DistinctTactics = array_length(Tactics)
| where DistinctTactics >= 2
   and Tactics has_any ("bcdedit","wbadmin")
| project FirstSeen, LastSeen, Computer, Account,
          DistinctTactics, Tactics, Parents, Cmds, EvCount
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Per CISA AA22-321A and Microsoft's BlackByte 2.0 analysis, BlackByte affiliates execute a near-identical pre-encryption recovery-kill sequence in nearly every documented intrusion. Other ransomware (LockBit, Conti, BlackCat) typically only use vssadmin+wmic; the addition of bcdedit boot-config tampering AND wbadmin catalog deletion within the same 10-minute window on a non-machine account is uncom
